slides.title: Alcohol stalls fat loss — *but not how you think*.
slides.sub: It's not really the liquid calories. It's what your body does the second the drink hits.

slides.title: Your body burns it first
slides.body: Alcohol is a toxin, so your body *burns it before anything else*. Fat-burning pauses until the booze is cleared.

slides.title: The rest gets stored
slides.body: While it handles the alcohol, the food you ate alongside it *has nowhere to go but storage*. The pizza isn't the problem — the timing is.

slides.title: It wrecks your REM
slides.body: A nightcap knocks you out but *blocks deep and REM sleep*. You wake unrested even after eight hours.

slides.title: Then you're starving
slides.body: Poor sleep plus a blood-sugar dip leaves you *ravenous and craving carbs* the whole next day. The damage is the day after.

slides.title: Cap it, don't quit it
slides.body: You don't have to go dry. *A couple of alcohol-free days a week* can support steadier sleep, hunger and progress.

slides.title: Drink smarter, *recover faster*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your drinks and connects them to your sleep and next-day hunger — so you see the real cost in one score.
slides.tag: see what the drink really costs

caption:
Alcohol doesn't stall fat loss because of the calories — it's that your body burns it first and parks everything else. 🍺

It also flattens your REM and leaves you starving the next day. The fix isn't quitting — it's a couple of dry days a week. 🔖

Vibe Health connects your drinks to your sleep and next-day hunger → link in bio.
youtube_title: What Alcohol Really Does to Fat Loss
